comprehensive_summary: Keri Hulme is a New Zealand author identified in quizbowl clues as asexual. She is best known for the bone people, a novel in which a fictional stand-in for the author intervenes to stop an alcoholic from assaulting his mute son. Clues also mention BAIT, an unfinished novel about fishing that Hulme never completed before her death. The available clue data for Hulme is extremely limited, with only a single bonus part referencing her, so the picture drawn from clues alone is narrow: a New Zealand asexual author, the bone people as her major work, and BAIT as an unfinished project.
discarded_note: Discarded B2 Part 3 about T. E. Hulme (the Imagist critic), a completely different person.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Keri Hulme was a New Zealand author who identified as asexual. She passed away without completing her novel BAIT, which was about fishing. These biographical facts appear alongside her major work the bone people in quizbowl clues.
